title: ophioglossum lusitanicum subsp. californicum
intro:
Ophioglossum lusitanicum subsp. californicum, also known as California adder's tongue fern, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the genus Ophioglossum, a group of ferns characterized by their unique, tongue-shaped fronds.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Ophioglossum lusitanicum subsp. californicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, this plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
